params.name: Royal Game of Ur
params.description:
On Apr 17, 1916 AD, British archaeologist Sir Leonard Woolley discovered a set of chessboards in Iraq.
The Royal Game of Ur is the oldest game board discovered to date. Made of shells, the board is composed of twenty squares arranged in groups of five. The aim of the game is for both players to move their pieces across the board as quickly as possible. Today, there are still variations of this game played in some southern Indian communities.
